college_types.regulatory_body: AICTE (All India Council for Technical Education)

college_types.duration: MBBS: 5.5 years, BDS: 5 years, B.Pharm: 4 years, Nursing: 4 years
college_types.regulatory_body: NMC (National Medical Commission), DCI (Dental Council), PCI (Pharmacy Council)
college_types.grading: Pass/Fail with marks percentage; 50% pass mark (45% for SC/ST)
college_types.typical_features: Clinical rotations, internship mandatory, hands-on patient care training

college_types.grading: CGPA or percentage-based; semester or annual pattern
college_types.exam_pattern: Annual or semester system depending on university

college_types.admission: Through CAT, MAT, XAT, GMAT, CMAT, state-level MBA entrance exams
college_types.typical_features: Case studies, industry projects, summer internship mandatory, placement cell very active

college_types.duration: 5 years (integrated BA LLB), 3 years (LLB after graduation), 1-2 years (LLM)
college_types.admission: Through CLAT, AILET, LSAT India, state law entrance exams
college_types.typical_features: Moot court practice, internships with advocates/courts, legal aid clinics

college_types.regulatory_body: Council of Architecture (COA), NID (National Institute of Design)
college_types.typical_features: Studio work, design portfolio, site visits, jury presentations

college_types.admission: Through ICAR AIEEA, state agricultural university entrance exams
college_types.typical_features: Farm practicals, field visits, research projects, RAWE (Rural Agricultural Work Experience)

college_types.admission: Through state B.Ed entrance exams, CTET, TET for teacher jobs

autonomous_vs_affiliated.autonomous: Sets its own syllabus, conducts its own exams, grants degrees in its own name or through a university. Example: BITS Pilani, VIT, Manipal.
autonomous_vs_affiliated.affiliated: Follows parent university's syllabus and exam schedule. Degrees awarded by the parent university. Most government and private colleges fall in this category.
autonomous_vs_affiliated.deemed_university: UGC-granted status allowing institution to function as an independent university. Example: Amity University, SASTRA University.
autonomous_vs_affiliated.central_university: Established by Central Government. Example: Delhi University, JNU, BHU.
autonomous_vs_affiliated.state_university: Established by State Government. Example: Mumbai University, Osmania University.
autonomous_vs_affiliated.national_importance: Institutions of National Importance: IITs, NITs, IISc, IIMs, AIIMS.

accreditation_bodies.NAAC: National Assessment and Accreditation Council - grades colleges A++, A+, A, B++, B+, B, C, D
accreditation_bodies.NBA: National Board of Accreditation - accredits engineering & management programs
accreditation_bodies.NIRF: National Institutional Ranking Framework - annual ranking by MHRD/Ministry of Education
accreditation_bodies.IET: Institution of Engineering and Technology accreditation
